project_specification.project_goal: Make the CauseFlow monorepo present and behave as a credible open-source SRE AI tool: commercial sales surfaces (pricing, plan selection, early-access/founder funnels, billing/credits) are gone; marketing CTAs are Docs, GitHub, and a quiet Dashboard link; Settings lets admins point investigations at any OpenAI-compatible LLM (Ornith local presets included) with an explicit fallback chain; Integrations ships Test Application (OSS) with public-docs explaining the fixed demo failure catalog; and a local-auth Playwright/API golden path proves Test Application → Ornith 9B → Incidents → full investigation workflow with evidence grounded in that catalog. Prior web/core OSS Acceptance Checks remain historical append-only records; this root Completion Contract is the sole proof for this goal.
project_specification.overview: CauseFlow is a multi-project monorepo (web marketing + dashboard, core control-plane API/workers, public-docs, relay). This root plan finishes the open-source productization across those surfaces without inventing a replacement stack. Operators run Docker Compose locally, authenticate with Core local JWT auth (not Clerk staging), connect the Core-owned causeflow-test-app, configure Ornith 9B (or another OpenAI-compatible profile) in Settings, and use Incidents to exercise triage → investigation → root cause → remediation against a documented demo failure catalog (order-service pool exhaustion, payment-service timeout, elevated CPU/latency, deploy v2.4.1-ac058). The website no longer sells plans or early access; public docs describe free self-host usage and the Test Application catalog.

term.name: Test Application (OSS)
term.avoid: stub mock only, Playwright fixture, Composio demo

glossary.term: The Core-owned runnable HTTP service (causeflow-test-app on :5190) that operators connect from Integrations as a real upstream. It emits demo alerts and returns deterministic investigation evidence for the fixed failure catalog.
A named tenant-scoped OpenAI-compatible connector (label, baseUrl, model, optional API key, optional contextWindowTokens, optional fallbackProfileId) stored by Core and activated for investigations.

The fixed, documented set of intentional issues in Test Application (OSS): order-service DB pool exhaustion, payment-service upstream timeout, high CPU / elevated p99, degraded health, and deploy v2.4.1-ac058 before alert onset.
Core register/login JWT authentication used by the local OSS dashboard runtime and golden-path E2E.

term.name: Commercial surface
term.avoid: usage-only billing page kept for OSS

glossary.term: Pricing pages, plan selection, early-access/waitlist/founder CTAs, Stripe/credits/quota UX, and related APIs that sell or meter a hosted product.

context.name: OSS marketing identity
context.responsibility: web website chrome and copy present CauseFlow as an open-source project (Docs + GitHub + quiet Dashboard).
context.relationships: Downstream of public-docs for Learn more / docs URLs; no dependency on billing.

context.name: OSS dashboard product path
context.responsibility: web dashboard removes commercial onboarding/billing, exposes custom Investigation LLM profiles, and surfaces Test Application (OSS) with docs deep-link.
context.relationships: Downstream of core Investigation LLM APIs and stub-upstream connect/probe/ingest; upstream of golden-path E2E.

context.name: OSS control plane
context.responsibility: core deletes commercial billing/credits/plan runtime on the OSS path, persists Investigation LLM profiles + fallback chain, and owns causeflow-test-app contracts.
context.relationships: Upstream of web BFF; integrates Ornith/OpenAI-compatible LLM clients; emits incidents for the demo catalog.

context.name: OSS public docs
context.responsibility: public-docs documents free self-host usage and the Test Application demo failure catalog / golden path.
context.relationships: Linked from web Integrations card; must not cite paid plans or live pricing.

context.name: OSS golden path proof
context.responsibility: Cross-project E2E proving Test Application + Ornith + Incidents full workflow with local auth.
context.relationships: Depends on web-oss-dashboard and core-oss-runtime behaviors.

technology_stack.general: Existing causeflow-ai monorepo. Do not invent a replacement stack. Registered projects: web, core, public-docs, relay. Root docker-compose umbrella runs website :3000, dashboard :3001, Core API :3099, postgres, redis, hindsight, worker, causeflow-test-app :5190, docs as already wired for OSS. pnpm workspaces; Conventional Commits. This plan is existing-codebase: verify-first where already implemented, repair/implement only where Acceptance Checks fail.

frontend.framework: web: Next.js 15 App Router (website SSG + dashboard SSR) as already in web/
frontend.styling: Existing Tailwind + @causeflow/ui themes — preserve visual language; change copy/routes/CTAs, not a redesign
frontend.state_management: Existing React 19 / Next patterns; dashboard BFF to Core
frontend.routing: Website and dashboard App Router with next-intl EN + PT-BR; hard-remove commercial routes (404)
frontend.port: Website host :3000, dashboard host :3001 via compose/dev as today
frontend.patterns: Existing DDD bounded contexts in web; thin app routes; no Playwright page.route mocks as golden-path pass path

backend.runtime: core: Node.js 22 + Hono modular monolith as today
backend.database: OSS Postgres persistence already used by local runtime; Investigation LLM profiles persist in Core (not dashboard). Prefer delete commercial billing/credits tables/use-cases on OSS product path rather than leaving zombie APIs.
backend.patterns: Clean Architecture modules; OpenAI-compatible LLM client for Ornith; stub-upstream integration use cases for Test Application

technology_stack.communication: REST/JSON Core /v1 + dashboard /api BFF; SSE investigation progress; test-app webhooks into Core Datadog-shaped ingest; public-docs static/Mintlify site for operator docs.

integration.name: Ornith 9B (OpenAI-compatible llama.cpp)
integration.responsibility: Default local Investigation LLM for OSS demos via OpenAI-compatible API (typically host :8081, model Ornith-1.0-9B-code).
integration.availability: required for golden-path E2E when proving Ornith; optional if another profile is active
integration.configuration: Configured as an Investigation LLM profile (baseUrl/model); presets prefill Settings form. Env defaults may seed first-run but Settings profiles are the product path.
integration.security: Optional API key stored by Core, never returned plaintext on GET.
integration.failure_behavior: Follow optional fallbackProfileId chain; if exhausted, fail closed with clear configure/fix-LLM error. No silent DeterministicLLMClient or Anthropic success path.
integration.deployment: Operator host llama.cpp / documented session script; not bundled as a paid SaaS dependency.
integration.affected_surfaces: core LLM client + health; web Settings Investigation LLM; golden-path E2E

integration.name: CauseFlow Test Application (OSS)
integration.responsibility: Runnable demo upstream (core/test-app) exposing connect/probe/tools/alerts for the fixed demo failure catalog.
integration.availability: required for OSS compose golden path
integration.configuration: STUB_UPSTREAM_BASE_URL to Docker DNS hostname (e.g. http://causeflow-test-app:5190), not container localhost.
integration.security: Tenant-scoped connect mapping; signed webhooks into Core ingest.
integration.failure_behavior: If unreachable, Connect/Test fail with clear unreachable error and never mark healthy/connected success.
integration.deployment: Always started with default OSS compose as causeflow-test-app :5190.
integration.affected_surfaces: core stub integration APIs; web Integrations card; Incidents golden path; public-docs catalog page

integration.name: Stripe / commercial billing
integration.security: No Stripe secrets in OSS web or Core runtime
integration.affected_surfaces: web billing context, Core billing module, marketing pricing, public-docs paid plan narrative

integration.name: Clerk staging E2E
integration.responsibility: removed as pass path for this Completion Contract
integration.failure_behavior: E2E must not depend on Clerk staging credentials
integration.affected_surfaces: web Playwright OSS project; dashboard auth in OSS runtime

integrations.contradictions: Existing web AC-078 expected /pricing redirect to docs; this root contract supersedes with hard 404. Existing public-docs billing pages still describe 410/usage billing screens; this contract requires hard-remove narrative aligned with deleted billing UI. Prior web/core OSS ACs remain in those project_specs.xml files as history; root ACs are the sole Completion Contract for this goal.

prerequisites.environment_setup: - Work at monorepo Git root; changes land in web/, core/, public-docs/ (and root compose) as named by each Acceptance Check. - Docker Compose OSS umbrella available; Core API, worker, postgres, redis, hindsight, causeflow-test-app, website :3000, dashboard :3001. - Ornith 9B reachable for golden-path proof (operator host OpenAI-compatible endpoint). - No Stripe/Clerk-staging credentials required for the Completion Contract. - Prefer editing existing OSS WIP rather than inventing parallel stacks. - Wide unrelated dead-code archaeology is deferred; only commercial product-path purge is in scope.

core_features.foundation: - Monorepo OSS compose keeps causeflow-test-app running with STUB_UPSTREAM_BASE_URL on Docker DNS. - Root plan is the sole Completion Contract for OSS productization; subproject checks name web/core/public-docs. - Runtime blockers (commercial deps forcing paid SaaS) stay removed for local smoke.
core_features.web-oss-marketing: - (web) Remove Pricing from header/footer/nav (EN + PT-BR). - (web) /pricing and /pt-br/pricing hard-remove → 404 (no redirect, no plan cards). - (web) Primary CTAs are Docs and GitHub; quiet Dashboard link allowed in chrome; no Get Early Access / Start Free / See Pricing / Talk to the Co-Founder sales funnel. - (web) Replace CauseFlow AI, LLC sales chrome with OSS project identity; scrub early-access/limited-spots copy. - (web) /get-started and equivalent early-access entry points hard-remove → 404.
core_features.web-oss-dashboard: - (web) /onboarding/choose-plan hard-remove → 404; post create-organization lands on dashboard home. - (web) Hard-remove Billing nav, credits/investigations-left chrome; /dashboard/billing → 404. - (web) Remove waitlist/beta-waitlist commercial funnel routes (404). - (web) Settings Investigation LLM: admin CRUD for OpenAI-compatible profiles + activate + optional fallbackProfileId; Ornith local/API presets prefill only. - (web) Non-admins can view active provider label but cannot mutate profiles. - (web) Integrations shows Test Application (OSS) with short scope summary + Learn more deep-link to public-docs catalog page.
core_features.core-oss-runtime: - (core) Prefer delete Stripe/checkout/portal/credits/plan-catalog/early-access APIs and runtime on OSS product path (not a long-lived 410 facade). - (core) Persist Investigation LLM profiles (secrets redacted on GET); honor active profile + fallbackProfileId chain with cycle protection; exhausted chain fails closed. - (core) Own causeflow-test-app contract: connect, probe, ingest, tool evidence for the fixed demo failure catalog. - (core) Unreachable test-app fails connect/probe clearly; never false healthy success.
core_features.public-docs-oss: - (public-docs) Canonical Test Application (OSS) page: scope, fixed demo failure catalog, golden-path steps, link target for Integrations Learn more. - (public-docs) Remove paid plan / Stripe checkout / choose-a-plan narrative; describe free self-host OSS; no Dashboard Billing screen guidance.
core_features.oss-golden-path: - Local-auth E2E: connect Test Application → activate Ornith local profile (with optional fallback configured) → emit/ingest demo alert → Incidents shows incident → investigation completes with catalog-grounded evidence → root-cause + remediation visible. - No Clerk staging; no Playwright mocks of integrations/incidents as pass path.

acceptance_check.description: (monorepo-root + core) Default OSS compose starts causeflow-test-app healthy and configures Core STUB_UPSTREAM_BASE_URL with the Docker service hostname (not 127.0.0.1 inside the API container). A health check against the test app succeeds on the compose network.
(web) On EN and PT-BR marketing pages, header/footer/nav have no Pricing item and shared nav constants do not list Pricing.
(web) GET /pricing and /pt-br/pricing return 404 (or framework not-found) and never render paid plan cards or redirect to a sales pricing page.

(web) Homepage and primary product marketing surfaces use Docs and/or GitHub as the primary CTA group, may include a quiet Dashboard link, and do not present Get Early Access, Start Free, See Pricing, or Talk to the Co-Founder as conversion CTAs (EN + PT-BR).

(web) Marketing chrome no longer presents CauseFlow AI, LLC company-sales branding; OSS project identity (CauseFlow) plus Docs/GitHub framing is used instead. Privacy/Terms may remain if still valid legal pages.

(web) /get-started and /pt-br/get-started (and equivalent early-access entry redirects to commercial sign-up) are hard-removed and return 404 rather than sending users to staging/commercial early-access funnels.
(web) Authenticated visit to /onboarding/choose-plan returns 404 and never renders Starter/Pro/Business plan cards or checkout actions.
(web) After OSS local-auth sign-up and create-organization, the user lands on dashboard home (or overview) without being forced through choose-plan or billing.
(web) Dashboard shell has no Billing nav and no credits/investigations-left widget; /dashboard/billing returns 404 and never shows plan cards, Buy more, or quota packs.
(web) /waitlist and /beta-waitlist (and locale equivalents) are hard-removed (404) and do not present early-access waitlist product UX.

(core) OSS product path prefers delete: Stripe clients, checkout/portal/credits reservation, and plan-catalog enforcement used by the dashboard are removed (not relied on as a permanent 410 facade). Creating an investigation is not blocked by CREDITS_EXHAUSTED / free-tier quota.
(web) Dashboard billing API routes used for checkout/portal/credits are removed (404) or absent; investigation create via UI is not blocked by a local credits ledger.

(web + core) Admin can create an Investigation LLM profile (label, baseUrl, model, optional API key, optional contextWindowTokens, optional fallbackProfileId) via Settings; profile persists via Core and appears after reload.
(web + core) Admin can edit/delete profiles and activate exactly one; API keys are never returned plaintext after save (masked/redacted only).
(web) Ornith (local) and Ornith (API) example presets prefill the create form; they are helpers, not a locked three-provider enum catalog.
(web + core) Non-admin users cannot create/edit/delete/activate Investigation LLM profiles (UI disabled/absent and API 403 or equivalent).
(web + core) Active profile cannot be deleted until another is activated; clear error otherwise.

(core) When the active Investigation LLM fails (e.g. Ornith down), Core follows optional fallbackProfileId chain (max depth / cycle-safe). If the chain is missing or exhausted, investigation fails closed with a clear configure/fix-LLM error and does not silently succeed via DeterministicLLMClient or Anthropic.

(web + core) Missing required profile fields are rejected with validation errors; optional API key may be omitted for local Ornith; connectivity probe is soft and does not block save/activate when the endpoint is temporarily down.
(web) Integrations lists Test Application (OSS) with a short scope summary and a Learn more link to the public-docs Test Application page (AC-023).
(web + core) Authenticated Connect for Test Application (OSS) succeeds when the test app is healthy and persists a connected integration named Test Application (OSS).
(web + core) When causeflow-test-app is stopped, Connect or Test fails with a clear unreachable message and does not mark the integration healthy/connected successfully.

(public-docs) A canonical Test Application (OSS) docs page explains scope, the fixed demo failure catalog (pool exhaustion, payment timeout, CPU/latency/health degradation, deploy v2.4.1-ac058), and the golden-path steps operators use with Incidents.

(public-docs) Docs no longer present paid tiers, Stripe checkout, choose-a-plan, or Dashboard Billing as the OSS product path; they describe free self-host usage and point to Docs/GitHub/self-host instructions.

(web + core) Golden-path E2E with OSS local auth only: activate Ornith (local) profile → connect Test Application → emit/ingest the demo alert → Incidents shows the incident → investigation runs → evidence/root-cause references the demo failure catalog → remediation artifact is visible. Clerk staging and Playwright mocks of /api/integrations or /api/incidents are not the pass path.
(web + core) A single documented Playwright project and/or Core verify script is the harness QA gate for AC-025 and exits 0 on the local OSS stack with Ornith available.

decision.question: Where should this cross-cutting OSS Completion Contract live?
decision.options: 1) Git-root root project full contract 2) Gaps-only append on web 3) Split web/core without root
decision.choice: Git-root root project owns the full Completion Contract; prior web/core OSS ACs remain historical append-only.
decision.rationale: User selected root ownership to avoid fighting overlapping queues while covering web+core+docs.

decision.question: What happens at /pricing?
decision.options: 1) Redirect to docs 2) OSS free-forever page 3) Hard remove 404
decision.choice: Hard remove — 404, no Pricing nav, no redirect.
decision.rationale: User rejected sales pricing entirely, including redirect soft-landings.

decision.question: What replaces commercial marketing CTAs?
decision.options: 1) Docs+GitHub only 2) Docs+GitHub+quiet Dashboard 3) Soft community CTA
decision.choice: Docs + GitHub primary CTAs with a quiet Dashboard link; no early-access/founder/pricing funnel.
decision.rationale: Reads as OSS while operators can still open the local dashboard.

decision.question: What happens at /onboarding/choose-plan?
decision.options: 1) Redirect away 2) Hard remove 404 3) OSS welcome page at same URL
decision.choice: Hard remove 404; post-org lands on dashboard home.
decision.rationale: Consistent with pricing hard-remove; no plan product.

decision.question: How is Test Application explained and used?
decision.options: 1) Inline-only 2) Card + public-docs deep-link 3) Guided wizard
decision.choice: Short card copy + public-docs canonical page; operators use resulting Incidents to test the full workflow against the fixed demo catalog already in core/test-app.
decision.rationale: User chose docs deep-link and full workflow testing on the example project's issues.

decision.question: How are Investigation LLMs configured?
decision.options: 1) Custom OpenAI-compatible profiles 2) Env-only 3) Hybrid env+profiles
decision.choice: Custom OpenAI-compatible profiles with Ornith presets as helpers; optional fallbackProfileId chain on failure.
decision.rationale: OSS operators must point at any compatible endpoint; fallback is explicit, not hidden Anthropic.

decision.question: How aggressive is Core commercial dead-code cleanup?
decision.options: 1) Product-path purge prefer delete 2) Wide GC 3) 410 facade only
decision.choice: Product-path purge with prefer delete for billing/credits/plans/early-access APIs the OSS dashboard does not need.
decision.rationale: User chose prefer remove without repo-wide archaeology.

decision.question: Which auth is the golden-path E2E pass path?
decision.options: 1) OSS local JWT 2) Clerk staging 3) Either
decision.rationale: Matches self-host operators; Clerk staging is not the OSS proof.

decision.question: Ornith down / active LLM fails?
decision.options: 1) Fail closed only 2) Auto-fallback via fallbackProfileId chain 3) Auto Anthropic
decision.choice: Follow explicit fallbackProfileId chain (cycle-safe); exhausted → clear error; no silent stub/Anthropic success.
decision.rationale: User chose auto-fallback to another configured profile with explicit chain.

decision.question: Test app unreachable; billing bookmarks; non-admin LLM; funnel routes?
decision.choice: Unreachable test-app clear failure; billing/waitlist/get-started/choose-plan/pricing 404; non-admin cannot mutate LLM; soft probe on save; keys redacted.
decision.rationale: Recommended defaults accepted by user for remaining edge cases.

planning_decisions.deferred: Wide unrelated dead-code garbage collection outside commercial product path — deferred to a later feature to avoid risky monorepo churn. Automatic Anthropic fallback when no OpenAI-compatible fallback profile exists — deferred; operators must configure fallbackProfileId explicitly. Expanding the demo failure catalog beyond the existing core/test-app set — deferred. Community Discord/Discussions CTA as primary marketing action — deferred; Docs+GitHub+quiet Dashboard only.

tables.investigation_llm_profiles: - id, tenant_id, label, base_url, model, api_key_encrypted (nullable), context_window_tokens (nullable), fallback_profile_id (nullable), created_at, updated_at
tables.integrations_stub_upstream: - existing Core integration records for provider stub-upstream / Test Application (OSS); no new commercial billing tables
tables.commercial_billing_tables: - removed from OSS product path (prefer delete credits/subscription enforcement used by dashboard)

project_specification.api_endpoints_summary: Core (OSS product path): - CRUD /v1/settings/investigation-llm/profiles (names may match existing routes) - POST /v1/settings/investigation-llm/profiles/:id/activate - POST /v1/integrations/stub/connect - POST /v1/integrations/stub/probe - POST /v1/integrations/stub/ingest (or test-app alerts/emit orchestration) - Incident list/detail + investigation SSE as today - Commercial billing checkout/portal/credits endpoints removed (prefer delete) Dashboard BFF mirrors the above under /api/... Website: no /pricing route (404)

ui_layout.main_structure: Website: existing marketing layout without Pricing nav; hero CTA group Docs + GitHub; quiet Dashboard in chrome. Dashboard: existing shell without Billing/credits chrome; Settings hosts Investigation LLM profiles card; Integrations hosts Test Application (OSS) card with Learn more; Incidents remains the investigation workspace.

design_system.color_palette: Preserve existing CauseFlow website and dashboard themes in packages/ui — no new brand system.
design_system.components: Reuse existing Shadcn/ui primitives; copy and route changes only unless a control is required for LLM profile CRUD.
design_system.animations: Preserve existing motion; do not add sales confetti/onboarding plan celebrations tied to commercial plans.

project_specification.key_interactions: 1) Visitor opens localhost:3000 → sees OSS CTAs (Docs/GitHub) → no pricing/early-access funnel; /pricing 404s. 2) Operator signs in with OSS local auth on :3001 → after org bootstrap lands on dashboard home → opens Integrations → reads Test Application scope → Learn more opens public-docs catalog → Connect succeeds. 3) Operator opens Settings → applies Ornith (local) preset → saves/activates profile (optional fallbackProfileId) → returns to Integrations/Incidents. 4) Demo alert ingested from Test Application → Incidents lists incident → investigation runs on Ornith (or fallback) → catalog-grounded root cause + remediation shown.

step.title: Foundation: compose + test-app wiring
step.tasks: - Verify root/core compose starts causeflow-test-app and STUB_UPSTREAM_BASE_URL (AC-001).

step.title: web commercial surface hard-remove
step.tasks: - Remove pricing nav/routes, sales CTAs, LLC chrome, get-started (AC-002..AC-006). - 404 choose-plan, billing, waitlist/beta; post-org → dashboard home (AC-007..AC-010, AC-012).

step.title: core commercial purge + LLM profiles + test-app
step.tasks: - Delete OSS-path billing/credits/plan runtime (AC-011). - Ensure Investigation LLM profile APIs + fallback chain (AC-013..AC-019). - Ensure stub connect/probe/ingest + unreachable errors (AC-021, AC-022).

step.title: public-docs catalog + OSS narrative
step.tasks: - Add/update Test Application docs page and scrub paid billing narrative (AC-023, AC-024). - Wire Integrations Learn more to that page (AC-020).

step.title: Golden-path E2E gate
step.tasks: - Implement/repair local-auth Playwright +/or Core verify script for full workflow (AC-025, AC-026).

success_criteria.functionality: localhost:3000 has no pricing/early-access sales funnel; localhost:3001 has no choose-plan/billing; Settings configures arbitrary OpenAI-compatible LLMs; Test Application connects; Incidents golden path works with Ornith and the documented catalog.
success_criteria.user_experience: Site reads as an open-source SRE project (Docs/GitHub), not a SaaS launch waitlist.
success_criteria.technical_quality: Commercial product-path APIs removed in Core/web; secrets redacted; E2E uses local auth only; no silent LLM stub success.
success_criteria.design_polish: Existing design system preserved; copy/nav consistency EN+PT-BR; 404s for removed commercial routes.
